titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the New Haven East Association Records
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 11, 2018 at 04:07

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains bound volumes of manuscript meeting minutes for the New Haven East Association and the Consociation of the County of New Haven, New Haven East Consociation, Association of the County of New-Haven, and New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers. Also present are other records and papers related to the business of the association, its member churches and foreign missions, its mutual improvement societies, and a painted metal box that formerly held the records.

corpname.part: Association of the County of New-Haven (Conn.)
Consociation of the County of New-Haven (Conn.)
New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the New Haven East Association, 1887-1903.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The New Haven East Association Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: New Haven East Association Records. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The records in this collection had been arranged by the association before being transferred to the Yale University Library. That arrangement was retained when the records were rehoused in 2015.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: MS Vault New Haven East

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: New Haven West Association Records (GEN MSS 1296), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: New Haven East Association
bioghist.p: The Association of the County of New-Haven, an organization of Connecticut Congregational ministers, was formed in 1709 following a meeting of twelve ministers and four church laymen called by the colonial legislature. Held in Saybrook, Connecticut, the meeting resulted in the "Saybrook Platform," a new constitution for the Congregational Church in Connecticut, which gave associations the responsibility to examine candidates for the ministry, and to oversee the behavior of the ministers. In 1787 the association was divided to form the New Haven East Association and the New Haven West Association, with the course of the Quinnipiac River as the geographical line. The two organizations were again united by unanimous resolution in 1893 to form the New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers in Connecticut. That organization merged with the New Haven Central Association in 1903.

The Consociation of the County of New-Haven, an organization of Congregational churches and pastors also formed in 1709, was devoted to the management of ecclesiastical discipline. That organization, with a membership composed of both church members and pastors, could impose discipline on specific churches and judge disputes that arose. It later evolved into two separate bodies called the New Haven East Consociation (established in 1787) and the Consociation of the Western District of New Haven County (established in 1799).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains bound volumes of manuscript meeting minutes for the New Haven East Association and the Consociation of the County of New Haven, New Haven East Consociation, Association of the County of New-Haven, and New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers. Also present are other records and papers related to the business of the association, its member churches and foreign missions, its mutual improvement societies, and a painted metal box that formerly held the records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Minute Books, 1737-1903. II. Papers of Regional Churches, 1758-1850. III. Other Papers, 1725-1903.

geogname.part: New Haven County (Conn.) -- Religious life and customs.

Minute books -- Connecticut -- New Haven County -- 19th century.
Minute books -- Connecticut -- New Haven County -- 20th Century.

Clergy -- Connecticut -- New Haven County -- 19th century.

corpname.part: Association of the County of New-Haven (Conn.)
Consociation of the County of New-Haven (Conn.)
New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into five subseries: Consociation of the County of New Haven, New Haven East Consociation, Association of the County of New-Haven, New Haven East Association, and New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains nine bound volumes of manuscript meeting minutes.

did.unittitle: Consociation of the County of New Haven
Association of the County of New-Haven
Meeting minutes, 1737-1757 (manuscript copy made in the nineteenth century)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume also contains meeting minutes of the New Haven East Consociation (1803-1823)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume also contains meeting minutes of the New Haven East Consociation (1824-1838)

did.unittitle: New Haven Union Association of Congregational Ministers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is arranged in alphabetical order by town.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains papers related to churches in towns within the New Haven East Association.

did.unittitle: Papers relating to the case of Rev. A. C. Collins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains other records and papers related to the business of the association and the consociation, including those regarding foreign missions, cases regarding specific clergy members, and the minutes of meetings. Also present are the minutes of two groups of clergy who met for "mutual improvement" and a painted metal box in which the records were previously stored.

did.unittitle: Minutes of Council for dismission of Rev. Mr. Gemmill from the North Church in New Haven
Papers relating to the Convention of Congregational and Presbyterian Ministers
Minutes of annual meetings of the New Haven East Consociation
Minutes of special meetings of the New Haven East Consociation

Minutes of semi-annual meetings of the New Haven East Association
Papers belonging to the Foreign Mission Society of Eastern District of New Haven County
Papers relating to the General Association of Connecticut
Dissertations on various topics received before the New Haven East Association
Miscellaneous papers belonging to the New Haven East Consociation
Miscellaneous papers belonging to the New Haven East Consociation
Miscellaneous papers belonging to the New Haven East Association
Records of the minutes of the meetings....for mutual improvement in the County of New Haven
Minutes of the circular meeting of the society for mutual improvement
Painted metal box formerly holding records
